title: Crumbly Smoky Ukrainian Prawn Pie
description: A nostalgic Ukrainian-inspired pie that balances earthy dill, smoky paprika, and a crumbly, buttery crust, perfect for a Mediterranean-friendly dinner.

ingredients.name: Raw frozen prawns, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: thawed to room temperature before cooking

ingredients.name: Cold butter (or 2 tbsp olive oil for dairy-free)
Egg, beaten (for egg wash) or 2 tbsp olive oil for vegan

instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, butter cubes, and a pinch of salt. Using a pastry blender or your fingers, cut the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add ice water a tablespoon at a time, mixing until dough just comes together. Wrap in plastic and chill for 15 minutes.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes. Add garlic and cook for another 30 seconds.

Add the thawed prawns to the skillet. Cook, stirring, until they turn pink and opaque, about 4 minutes. Stir in smoked paprika, liquid smoke, dill, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.

Roll out chilled dough on a floured surface to fit a 9-inch pie dish. Place the dough in the dish, pressing into the corners. Spoon the prawn mixture into the crust. If desired, top with a second crust or crumble the remaining dough into small pieces and sprinkle over the filling. Brush the crust with beaten egg or olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25–30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is heated through. Let the pie rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve warm with a fresh side salad or a squeeze of lemon.
